expectations.text: Moderate anemia classified (Hb 8.5 is in 7-9.9 range)
expectations.evidence: Section 2A states: 'Classification: Moderate anemia (Hb 8.5 g/dL; WHO defines moderate anemia in pregnancy as Hb 7.0-9.9 g/dL)'
Section 3A table states: 'Regimen: 120 mg elemental iron daily (therapeutic dose)' with rationale 'Moderate anemia (Hb 8.5 g/dL) with declining trend despite prophylactic dose (60 mg/day)'

expectations.text: Pre-eclampsia risk classified as HIGH (nulliparity + BMI >30 + elevated BP + proteinuria)
expectations.evidence: Section 2B heading is 'Pre-eclampsia Risk -- HIGH' and lists risk factors: 'Nulliparity (first pregnancy)', 'Obesity (BMI >= 30)', 'Elevated blood pressure with upward trend', 'New and progressive proteinuria'
Section 3C states: 'Aspirin 75-150 mg (preferably 150 mg) daily at bedtime' with heading 'Low-Dose Aspirin -- INITIATE NOW'
Section 3B states: 'Regimen: 1.5-2.0 g elemental calcium daily in divided doses (e.g., 500 mg three times daily)'

expectations.text: Proteinuria flagged for monitoring and workup
expectations.evidence: Investigation table includes 'Repeat urine dipstick - Monitor proteinuria trend' and 'Consider urine protein-to-creatinine ratio (spot) or 24-hour urine protein - Quantify proteinuria if +1 persists or worsens'

Section 5D states: 'Delivery MUST occur at a facility with cesarean section and blood transfusion capability (this district hospital is appropriate)' and Section 5E: 'Delivering at this district hospital (not at home or lower-level facility)'

expectations.text: Multiple risks result in more frequent monitoring schedule
expectations.evidence: Section 7 states: 'Increased visit frequency recommended -- every 2 weeks until 36 weeks, then weekly, given risk profile' and return in '1-2 weeks' for BP/proteinuria recheck
